key_insight: GAIA learns geometry-aware operators that can handle forward, boundary value, and inverse PDE problems on arbitrary domains in a single pass by conditioning integral layers on learned domain tokens.
engineering_relevance: This enables faster surrogate modeling for complex PDE systems on irregular geometries, reducing the need for retraining, iterative solvers, or graph construction in practical engineering workflows.
method: Encodes boundary geometry and interior field information into compact geometry tokens.
Uses cross-attention to adapt integral transform kernels locally to geometric features.
Supports both same-domain forward tasks and cross-domain inverse/BVP tasks with one architecture.
evidence_signal: Evaluated on seven 2D/3D benchmarks, including four new or extended inverse/BVP settings, and achieved state-of-the-art results on every inverse and BVP task while staying competitive on forward problems.
watch_item: Performance may depend on how well geometry tokens generalize across unseen shapes and point resolutions, so robustness outside the evaluated benchmark families should be checked.
paper_title: GAIA: Geometry-Adaptive Operator Learning for Forward and Inverse Problems
